description_long: This object depicts a small group of birds in mid-flight, with wings outstretched and bodies streamlined for gliding. The birds have slender, elongated wings and relatively short tails, typical of urban-dwelling gulls or terns. Their plumage appears predominantly light gray or white, with darker accents on the wing tips and possibly the head. The beaks are short and straight, suited for generalist feeding. The legs are not clearly visible in flight, but the overall body proportions suggest a medium-sized bird commonly seen in city parks or near water. The arrangement and spacing of the birds evoke a natural flocking behavior often observed in urban skies.
description: A small flock of light-colored birds with outstretched wings, streamlined bodies, and dark-tipped feathers, captured in mid-flight. Their form and plumage suggest urban gulls or similar species.
description_view_0: Two birds are visible in flight, one with wings fully extended and the other with wings angled downward, both showing light bodies and dark-tipped wings.
description_view_1: Three birds are seen from below, with wings spread wide and bodies streamlined, highlighting the symmetry and spacing of the flock.
description_view_2: Three birds are visible, with one bird's wings fully outstretched and the others at different angles, emphasizing the variation in wing position during flight.
description_view_3: Two birds are clearly visible with wings extended, and a third is partially obscured by a diagonal line, possibly a wire, adding an urban context.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid light gray and white with dark wingtips
